Product Design

BCH Arquitectura

End-to-end design and development of a portfolio website for an architecture studio in Buenos Aires — translating an established Instagram identity into a structured, responsive digital experience without a clear brief from the client.

TypeClient Project
RoleUX Designer + Front-end Developer
ToolsFigma, React, CSS
Year2023
01 — Problem

A studio with a strong identity but no website

BCH Arquitectura had built a solid visual presence on Instagram — clean renders, consistent branding, and nearly 4,000 followers. But they had no website. Potential clients couldn't find structured information about their services, couldn't browse projects properly, and had no clear way to get in touch beyond Instagram DMs.

The real challenge: the client didn't come in with a clear brief. They knew they needed a website, but they weren't sure what they wanted, how to structure it, or what sections made sense for an architecture studio.

The brief (or lack of one)

  • Strong Instagram identity — but no translated direction for web
  • No defined information architecture or page structure
  • Client unsure about what content to include and in what order
  • No existing website — starting from zero
02 — Research & References

Understanding the space before proposing a solution

Since the client didn't have a clear vision, I took the lead on research. I analyzed competitor architecture studio websites to understand what information users expect, what sections are standard, and how to present projects effectively in this space.

I also studied the BCH Instagram account closely — their visual language (olive green, clean white backgrounds, render-heavy content), their content categories (Reformas, Araucarias, Vila Marí, Vistas 227), and their brand voice — to ensure the website felt like a natural extension of what they'd already built.

The Instagram identity

3,848 followers. A consistent visual language built around architectural renders, moodboards, and project documentation. The olive green color system and clean typography were already established — the website needed to honor and extend that.

03 — Information Architecture

Defining the structure the client couldn't define

Based on the research and several meetings with the client, I proposed a structure that covered all the key user needs: understand what the studio does, see their work, learn about the team, and get in touch. The client validated and refined the structure through our conversations.

Home — Hero + Services
→
Estudio — About
→
Proyectos — Gallery
→
Project Detail
→
Contacto

Key structural decisions

  • Services (Arquitectura, Diseño, Construcción) above the fold — visitors need to know immediately what the studio offers
  • Separate "Estudio" page — architecture clients want to know who they're hiring before they see the work
  • Project gallery with individual project pages — each project needed its own space to show process and photography
  • Contact page with multiple channels — email, WhatsApp, Instagram, and office location all in one place
03b — Wireframes

Low-fidelity structure before visual design

Before moving into visual design, I mapped out the key screens in low-fidelity wireframes to validate the structure and flow with the client. This helped align on layout decisions early without getting distracted by colors or typography.

Home — Nav, hero image, 3 services, studio snippet, CTA

Proyectos — Full project grid, 4 columns

Project detail — Hero image, title, meta, gallery

04 — Design & Build

Translating the Instagram identity to web

With the structure defined, I designed and built each section staying true to the established visual language — olive green as the primary accent, clean white backgrounds, generous whitespace, and the project photography as the hero element throughout.

Home — Hero image, services section (Diseño, Arquitectura, Construcción)

Estudio — Studio values and team description

Proyectos — Full project gallery, 4-column grid

Contacto — Email, Instagram, WhatsApp, Oficina

Prensa — Featured in publications and media

05 — Key Decisions

What was decided and why

Design decisions

  • Olive green from Instagram kept as primary accent — consistency with existing brand recognition
  • Photography-first layout — in architecture, the work speaks louder than any copy
  • Individual project pages — clients need to see the full scope and detail of each project
  • Minimal navigation — Home, Estudio, Proyectos, Contacto — no unnecessary complexity
  • Fully responsive — architecture clients browse on mobile before scheduling a meeting

What was discarded

  • Blog or news section — out of scope, no content strategy defined yet
  • Online quote form — client preferred direct contact over automated flows
  • Dark mode — inconsistent with the light, airy visual identity from Instagram
06 — Result

A digital home for an established studio

The final result was a fully responsive website that feels like a natural extension of BCH's Instagram identity — structured, clear, and photography-first. The client went from having no web presence to a professional portfolio site that supports their sales process and builds credibility with new clients.

5
Pages designed and built end to end
